info.name: Arbiter — LLM Gateway API
info.description:
Official Postman collection for **Arbiter**, the self-hosted OpenAI-compatible LLM gateway (v1.21.0).

## Setup
1. Set the collection variable `base_url` to your instance (e.g. `http://localhost:8080` or `https://your-host`).
2. Set `gateway_token` to a valid gateway token (from `GATEWAY_API_KEYS` in `.env`, or one minted at `/settings → Gateway Tokens`).
3. All `/v1/*` requests inherit Bearer auth automatically.

## Smart routing
Use `"model": "auto"` to let Arbiter pick the best model by request complexity. Pin a provider with `?vendor=` or a model with `?force_model=`. Bias routing with the `X-Arbiter-Priority` header.

Repo: https://github.com/koushikch7/arbiter

request.description: Smart auto-routing: Arbiter classifies the request and selects the best free-tier model. The response `model` field and `X-Arbiter-Model-Used` header reveal the actual model used.
Bias routing toward higher-quality (larger) models. Values: speed | quality | balanced.
Force a specific provider first (gemini, groq, cerebras, cloudflare, nvidia, openrouter, cohere, ollama, routeway, huggingface, pollinations). Falls back to others on failure.
Server-Sent Events stream of `chat.completion.chunk` objects, terminated by `data: [DONE]`.
Grounds the answer in live Tavily web results with numbered citations. Source URLs returned in `X-Arbiter-Realtime-Sources` header and `x_arbiter.realtime_sources`.
Pass an OpenAI-format `tools` array — Arbiter auto-routes to tool-capable providers (groq, nvidia, openrouter, cerebras, ollama) and forwards the definitions.
OpenAI-compatible model list across all configured providers.
Available image-generation models and supported sizes.
Free image generation via Pollinations.ai — no provider key required.
Liveness probe — returns status, provider list, Redis connectivity, and version. No auth required.

item.name: Admin (token & provider management)
item.description: Admin endpoints. When Google SSO is enabled these require a signed session cookie (use the browser UI). With token-only auth they accept the gateway Bearer token.

request.description: All providers with status, key counts, and pool stats.
Add a provider API key at runtime (written to .env, auto-tested). Replace `gemini` with any provider name.
List named gateway tokens with their routing policies and rate limits.
Mint a new named gateway token. Optional: per-token rate limit, routing policy (auto|restricted|preferred), allowed/blocked models.
Real-time analytics payload: KPIs, rolling rates, in-flight gauge, recent activity, per-provider/model stats.
